title: Subset Sum Problem

content.problemStatement:
You have NNN positive integers, ithi^{th}ith of them being AiA_iAi​ and an positive integer XXX. 

You need to answer if you can pick a subset of these elements such that the sum of the subset is equal to xxx.

HintJust like the 0/1 knapsack problem, we have to pick or not pick the integers, but instead of maximising the value, we need to check if we can make the required sum or not.


Input Format

The first line contains a two integers NNN and XXX, denoting the length of array AAA and the target sum value.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
Output `YES` if you can pick a subset with sum equal to XXX, otherwise output `NO`.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers N and X, the number of elements and the required sum. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if a subset of the given numbers sums exactly to X, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: subset {1,4} sums to 5 → YES. Test case 2: no subset sums to 8 → NO. Test case 3: subset {2,3} sums to 5 → YES.
Empty subset always gives sum 0, so answer is YES.

solution.approach: The problem is a classic 0/1 knapsack decision variant. We maintain a boolean DP array dp[s] that indicates whether a sum s can be obtained using some subset of the processed elements. Initialise dp[0] = true (empty subset). For each element a, iterate s from X down to a and set dp[s] = true if dp[s‑a] was true. After processing all numbers, dp[X] tells whether the required sum is achievable. This runs in O(N·X) time and O(X) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N * X) per test case, where N is the number of elements and X is the target sum.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(X) auxiliary space for the DP array.
